title: celtis occidentalis var. georgiana
intro: Celtis occidentalis var. georgiana is a lesser-known variety of the common hackberry tree, native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its smaller stature and more compact growth habit compared to the common hackberry. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Celtis occidentalis var. georgiana include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing for various ways to harness its medicinal properties.
